description: Posture-aware routing suite for future-aware-architecture. The suite checks whether the skill behaves like a manual-first architecture methodology: it should activate only for explicit architecture or technology-selection decisions, insist on problem-first framing, and redirect review, pure research, or direction-already-chosen work to the correct neighboring workflow.

assertion_definitions.routing_verdict_correct: The response makes the right top-level routing call: route to future-aware-architecture only for explicit architecture or technology-selection decisions, and decline or redirect adjacent requests.
assertion_definitions.manual_first_boundary_respected: For routed cases, the response keeps the skill in architecture-method mode rather than collapsing immediately into implementation, review, or generic research.
assertion_definitions.first_method_step_correct: For routed cases, the response identifies the correct first move, such as framing the problem, classifying the decision, or defining constraints before discussing tools.
assertion_definitions.neighboring_workflow_redirect_correct: For non-routed cases, the response points to the correct neighboring workflow such as implementation-planning, review, brainstorming, or deep-research.
assertion_definitions.research_escalation_noted_when_needed: When the in-scope request depends on fast-moving or domain-specific facts, the response notes that fresh evidence gathering is part of the architecture flow.
assertion_definitions.next_step_explicit: The response closes with a concrete next step such as start framing, gather constraints, or switch to the neighboring workflow.

cases.name: Explicit cross-project architecture decision should trigger
cases.description: A direct request for architecture and selection work. The skill should route and start with framing rather than technology answers.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Use future-aware-architecture to help us choose the system architecture and core technology stack for a new workflow platform. We need a real decision method, not implementation steps.' Using future-aware-architecture, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and what the next step is. Do not browse or edit files.

manual_first_boundary_respected.check: The answer should position the skill as an explicit architecture methodology rather than generic planning.

first_method_step_correct.check: The first step should be framing the problem, constraints, and decision shape before discussing stacks.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should say the next step is to define the architecture problem and criteria.

cases.name: Technology selection with fast-moving facts should trigger and note research
cases.description: A clear in-scope selection request that depends on current ecosystem facts.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Use future-aware-architecture to choose between several agent frameworks and our current in-house orchestration. Current ecosystem maturity matters.' Using future-aware-architecture, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and what the next step is. Do not browse or edit files.

manual_first_boundary_respected.check: The answer should keep the skill in architecture-selection mode, not jump straight into framework advocacy.

first_method_step_correct.check: The next step should be to frame the problem, decision criteria, and baseline options.

research_escalation_noted_when_needed.check: The answer should note that fresh evidence will likely be needed for the current ecosystem branch.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should clearly state the next action.

cases.name: Rollout plan request should not trigger
cases.description: The architecture is already chosen, so the skill should redirect to implementation planning.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'We already chose the architecture. Use future-aware-architecture to write the migration phases and verification checklist.' Using future-aware-architecture, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not browse or edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to future-aware-architecture.

neighboring_workflow_redirect_correct.check: The answer should redirect to implementation-planning.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should state that the next step is a planning workflow, not more architecture design.

cases.name: Pure latest-status research should not trigger
cases.description: The request asks only for current facts and sources, not an architecture decision.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Use future-aware-architecture to tell me the latest model and database rankings right now with sources.' Using future-aware-architecture, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not browse or edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to future-aware-architecture.

neighboring_workflow_redirect_correct.check: The answer should redirect to deep-research or a lighter current-state research flow.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name the research-oriented next step.

cases.name: Review of an existing architecture doc should not trigger
cases.description: The user wants critique, not architecture creation or selection.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Use future-aware-architecture to review our architecture doc and find risks.' Using future-aware-architecture, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not browse or edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to future-aware-architecture.

neighboring_workflow_redirect_correct.check: The answer should redirect to review.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name the review workflow as the next step.

cases.name: Pure ideation should not trigger
cases.description: The user is still asking what to build, so architecture methodology is premature.
cases.prompt: A user says: 'We want to do something with AI. What are some interesting product ideas?' Using future-aware-architecture, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not browse or edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to future-aware-architecture.

neighboring_workflow_redirect_correct.check: The answer should redirect to brainstorming because the user is still in idea exploration.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should point to the ideation step before architecture design.

scoring.notes: Use this suite as a manual routing review after description, boundary, or invocation-posture edits. A case passes if it scores at least 75% of the possible points for the assertions that apply to that case.

runner_instructions.setup: Ensure the canonical future-aware-architecture package is discoverable. These prompts are intentionally routing-focused and should not require editing files.
runner_instructions.execution: Run each prompt through a surface where the skill can activate. Capture whether the answer routes correctly, stays manual-first, and identifies the right first step or redirect.
runner_instructions.evaluation: Score each assertion as pass, partial, or fail after reading the captured response. Pay special attention to false positives on pure research, review, ideation, or rollout-planning requests.
runner_instructions.environment: Filesystem write access is useful for workspace artifacts, but the prompts themselves should stay read-only.
